title: Mapania macilentula
taxonomy: Mapania macilentula is classified as a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae. It belongs to the genus Mapania and is a type of monocotyledonous plant. The species is native to the tropical regions of the Old World, particularly in Southeast Asia. Further classification places Mapania macilentula within the order Poales and the kingdom Plantae. It is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within the Cyperaceae family.

Its leaves are traditionally used in folk medicine to alleviate respiratory issues. In some cultures, the plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Mapania macilentula, also known as the Mapania grass, is a species of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Mapania macilentula include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Mapania macilentula include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
